user_query:
I’m preparing a talk on the pacing and sonic intensity of classic Italian horror, and I want a small, reproducible local analysis from the scene annotations I’ve saved. Please implement a script or small program that does the following and produce the outputs exactly at the paths listed below.

1) Before coding, inspect both of these local files to guide the analysis:
   - config/analysis.yaml for parameters (especially the loudness threshold).
   - scripts/feature_spec.py for the exact expected CSV columns and their meanings. Use EXPECTED_COLUMNS from that file to validate each input.

2) Inspect the data/ directory to discover all scene CSVs (do not hardcode filenames). For each CSV under data/*.csv:
   - Validate that its header matches scripts/feature_spec.py:EXPECTED_COLUMNS exactly. If validation fails and config/analysis.yaml has strict_column_validation: true, abort with a clear error. (If validation passes, proceed.)
   - Compute per-film aggregates using all rows in that file:
     • total_duration_s = sum(end_time_s - start_time_s).
     • total_jump_scares = sum(jump_scare).
     • jumps_per_min = total_jump_scares / (total_duration_s / 60).
     • mean_scene_duration_s = average over scenes of (end_time_s - start_time_s).
     • median_shot_length_s = median over scenes of (end_time_s - start_time_s) / shot_count.
     • intense_loudness_proportion = (sum of durations for scenes where avg_loudness_db >= loudness_threshold_db from config/analysis.yaml) / total_duration_s.
     Also include scenes_analyzed and a data_valid boolean (true if columns matched the spec).

3) Write two structured result files:
   - outputs/pacing_summary.json: A JSON object with:
       • films: an array of per-film objects with the fields: film_title, total_duration_s, total_jump_scares, jumps_per_min, mean_scene_duration_s, median_shot_length_s, intense_loudness_proportion, scenes_analyzed, data_valid.
       • config_used: echo loudness_threshold_db from config/analysis.yaml.
   - outputs/film_comparison.csv: A CSV with one row per film and columns: film_title, jumps_per_min, median_shot_length_s, intense_loudness_proportion, ranking_by_jumps_per_min (1 is highest).

Notes and constraints:
- Use only the local files provided; do not fetch anything from the Internet.
- Do not hardcode input filenames; programmatically inspect the data/ directory for all .csv inputs.
- The thresholds and validation behavior must come from the supplied config/analysis.yaml and scripts/feature_spec.py.
- Make the analysis deterministic so another critic can rerun it on the same files and get the same outputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
